cyber_fraud.description: Unauthorized online transaction, UPI fraud, OTP scam, phishing, or bank fraud.
immediate_steps_0_24_hours: Immediately contact your bank customer support and request to freeze or block the affected account.
Call the national cybercrime helpline: 1930.
Report the fraud at https://cybercrime.gov.in.
Take screenshots of transaction details, SMS alerts, and bank statements.
Preserve evidence such as phone numbers, links, messages, or emails used by the scammer.
Visit the nearest police station and file a First Information Report (FIR).
Inform the UPI provider or payment app used (Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm etc).

expected_response_24_hours.bank_action: Bank may attempt to freeze the transferred funds if they are still in the destination account.
expected_response_24_hours.cybercrime_portal: Complaint is registered and assigned to cybercrime investigators.
expected_response_24_hours.police_station: Police should register FIR under IT Act and IPC fraud sections.

expected_response_48_hours.investigation_status: Cybercrime team may start tracing the receiving bank account and IP address used in the transaction.
expected_response_48_hours.bank_followup: Banks may initiate reversal process if funds are recoverable.
expected_response_48_hours.victim_expectation: Victim should receive acknowledgment of complaint and case reference number.

legal_sections_applied: IT Act Section 66D - Cheating by personation using computer resources

government_expected_action_time.FIR_registration: Immediately or within the same day.
government_expected_action_time.bank_account_trace: 3 to 7 days depending on bank response.

if_police_do_not_act.step_1: Send written complaint to Superintendent of Police (SP).
if_police_do_not_act.step_2: Submit complaint online to cybercrime portal again with FIR refusal evidence.
if_police_do_not_act.step_3: File complaint before Magistrate under CrPC Section 156(3).

mobile_theft.description: Phone stolen or lost; SIM/IMEI misuse; risk to bank accounts and personal data.
immediate_steps_0_24_hours: Call 112 immediately and report the theft at the nearest police station.
Contact your mobile provider (Airtel, Jio, Vodafone Idea) and request SIM blocking.
Use the CEIR portal or request police support to block the IMEI across all networks.
Contact your bank to freeze UPI payments, block debit/credit cards, and change passwords.
Enable Find My Device (if available) and attempt to locate/lock the phone remotely.
Keep notes of where and when the phone was stolen and any suspicious activity.

expected_response_24_hours.police_fir: Police should register FIR and record your statement.
expected_response_24_hours.cctv: Police should begin collecting nearby CCTV footage.
expected_response_24_hours.control_room: Police control room should be notified and start search operations.

expected_response_48_hours.phone_tracking: Authorities may attempt to track the phone via IMEI/GPS if active.
expected_response_48_hours.telecom_block: Telecom providers should block the IMEI and stop all network access.
expected_response_48_hours.police_alert: Nearby police stations should be alerted and patrols may be increased.

legal_sections_applied: IPC Section 381 - Dishonestly receiving stolen property

if_police_do_not_act.step_1: Contact Superintendent of Police (SP) with your FIR reference number.
if_police_do_not_act.step_2: File a petition to the District Magistrate asking for action.
if_police_do_not_act.step_3: File a complaint under CrPC Section 156(3) with the Magistrate.

immediate_steps_0_24_hours: Move to a safe location away from the abuser if possible.
Call emergency services at 112.
Call Women Helpline at 1091.
Seek help from trusted friends or relatives.
Visit nearest police station and file complaint.
Request protection order under Domestic Violence Act.

expected_response_24_hours.police_action: Police should register complaint and provide immediate protection if danger exists.
expected_response_24_hours.medical_help: Victim can receive medical support and documentation.

expected_response_48_hours.court_action: Protection order request may be processed.
expected_response_48_hours.police_followup: Police may question accused and gather evidence.

legal_sections_applied: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005
IPC Section 498A - Cruelty by husband or relatives

if_police_do_not_act.step_1: Report the issue to Superintendent of Police.

immediate_steps_0_24_hours: Move to a safe location.
Contact police helpline 112.
Save messages, photos, or evidence of harassment.
Report to nearest police station.

expected_response_48_hours.suspect_action: Police may summon or arrest suspect.

immediate_steps_0_24_hours: Request written reason for refusal.
Record date, time, and officer name.
Submit written complaint to Station House Officer.

expected_response_48_hours.escalation: Send complaint to Superintendent of Police.

legal_sections_applied: CrPC Section 156(3) - Magistrate power to order investigation

if_police_do_not_act.step_3: File complaint to Human Rights Commission.
